change_summary: model1d v1: characteristic decomposition labels swapped (q_out <-> q_in eigenvector combinations); no left-edge inflow BC; CCM run reused the outgoing initial pulse; R ratio missing the (1-b)/(1+b) amplitude factor
metric.name: rel_error
expected: q_in == 0 for pure outgoing data; R_Sommerfeld ~ 0.095
root_cause: for u = f(t - x/c+), Pi = -c+ Phi, so the outgoing-vanishing combination is Pi + (1+b)Phi (= the P1 BC operator, consistent with N5/N7) — my q_in/q_out assignments were the transposed eigenvector pair; additionally char amplitudes scale as 2/(1-b) vs 2/(1+b) so R_u = ratio*(1-b)/(1+b); left edge had no inflow BC
fix_hypothesis: swap definitions; add left-edge no-inflow BC Pi[0]=(1-b)Phi[0]; zero initial data for the injection run; correct the ratio factor

change_summary: model1d v2: BC reconstruction fixed but characteristic speed assignment still mirrored — assumed outgoing speed 1+b; the PDE factors as (d_t + (1-b)d_x)(d_t - (1+b)d_x), so outgoing (toward x=0) speed is 1-b and incoming 1+b for this sign convention
metric.name: rel_error
observed: identical results across BC kinds; left-eigenvector check (1, 1+b)A != lambda (1, 1+b) exposes the mirrored assignment
root_cause: orientation/sign: left eigenvectors of A = [[2b, 1-b^2],[1, 0]] are (1, 1-b) with speed 1+b (LEFT-moving = incoming) and (1, -(1+b)) with speed 1-b (RIGHT-moving = outgoing); analytic Sommerfeld R in this orientation is the b -> -b mirror of N5: b(1+b)/((1-b)(2+b))
fix_hypothesis: q_in = Pi + (1-b) Phi (target of the BC), q_out = Pi - (1+b) Phi (preserved); initial outgoing data Pi = -(1-b) Phi; datum 2 G'/(1+b); R = q_in/q_out with no extra factor; t_end = 20 (slower outgoing speed 0.7)
